name: One Credit Union Of Ny
description_short: One Credit Union is a New York-based credit union established in 1933, serving over 7,000 members with $81.9M in assets and offering comprehensive banking and lending services.
description_long:
One Credit Union was established in 1933 and is headquartered in Cheektowaga, New York. Operating under a state charter, it has evolved into a community-focused financial institution serving over 7,000 loyal members across western New York. The credit union maintains modest physical infrastructure with 1 branch location and 2 ATMs, complemented by digital banking capabilities for members who prefer remote access.

One Credit Union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, personal loans, auto loans, and mortgage options (fixed-rate mortgages up to 15 years and balloon/hybrid mortgages up to 5 years). Members also have access to modern banking services such as online bill pay, mobile banking apps (iOS and Android), remote deposit capture, e-statements, wire transfers, and drive-thru teller services. The institution provides IRA/KEOGH accounts, financial counseling, student scholarships, and multiple financial calculators to assist members with planning.

One Credit Union distinguishes itself as a member-owned cooperative with approximately 14 dedicated employees as of 2026. The institution maintains service status monitoring for online banking, mobile apps, and branch operations, allowing members to report and track service issues. Its longstanding presence since 1933 demonstrates stability and community commitment, while its emphasis on personal banking relationships sets it apart from larger regional institutions.

One Credit Union is best suited for individuals and families in the Cheektowaga/western New York area seeking traditional banking services from a community-oriented institution. The primary limitation is its small branch network (1 location, 2 ATMs), which may inconvenience members requiring frequent in-person services or traveling outside the local area. The institution's modest asset base ($81.9M) means it may have limitations in specialized lending products or competitive rates compared to larger credit unions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mortgage loans (fixed-rate 15 years or less, balloon/hybrid 5 years or less)
pros: Established 1933 with 90+ years of operational history and community presence
Member-owned cooperative structure with emphasis on personal service and community benefits
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, and mortgages
Modern digital banking: mobile app (iOS/Android), online bill pay, remote deposit capture, e-statements
Offers financial counseling and student scholarship programs beyond basic banking
Real-time service status monitoring for online banking, mobile app, and branch operations
Drive-thru teller service for convenient account access
cons: Very limited branch network (1 branch, 2 ATMs) restricts in-person service accessibility
Small asset base ($81.9M) may result in limited lending capacity and less competitive rates versus larger institutions
Website content indicates lobby and drive-thru are currently closed, raising questions about operational constraints
No user reviews available for the main office, limiting member feedback and transparency
Geographic charter limited to New York state membership eligibility
best_for: Cheektowaga and western New York residents seeking community-based banking from a locally-rooted institution
Members prioritizing personal banking relationships and financial counseling over digital-only banking
Individuals interested in auto loans or mortgage products from a stable, member-owned cooperative
Savers looking for basic deposit products (checking, savings, CDs) with a traditional credit union experience
diagnosis: One Credit Union is best for individuals in western New York seeking full-service banking from a community-focused, member-owned institution with 90+ years of history. The main caveat is the extremely limited branch footprint (1 location, 2 ATMs) and current operational constraints (lobby/drive-thru reported as closed), which may severely limit accessibility for members requiring frequent in-person services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
